claim_supported: thyssenkrupp nucera press release (fetched): Air Products (via the NEOM Green Hydrogen Company JV with NEOM and ACWA Power) awarded a contract for >2 GW of alkaline electrolysis capacity, built on thyssenkrupp's 20 MW module design; hydrogen is to be synthesized into carbon-free ammonia for exclusive export by Air Products; engineering/procurement had commenced with production originally expected to start in 2026.
locator: Press release body, fetched directly this session
notes: This specific press release does not itself state quantified hydrogen (600 t/day) or ammonia (1.2 Mt/yr) output targets, nor the ~4 GW renewable-capacity figure cited in the raw scout record and later secondary reporting -- those figures are dropped from this record's confirmed claim pending a separately-fetched source, per 'correct wrong metadata, never invent.'

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Same publisher as L11-048; headquartered Germany. Not India.

title: Ohmium opens second electrolyzer gigafactory in India
authors_or_org: pv magazine (trade press, reporting Ohmium International)
claim_supported: pv magazine article (fetched): Ohmium opened its second India gigafactory (Doddaballapura, near Bengaluru) in July 2024 with 2 GW/yr PEM-electrolyzer manufacturing capacity (expandable to 4 GW), producing fully assembled/tested PEM systems; named partnerships/contracts include a 400 MW deal with NTPC (2023, majority state-owned Indian utility), a 2024 strategic partnership with Tata Projects, and selection under India's MNRE SIGHT incentive program; also references a CSIR-CECRI/IIT Madras Research Park technology-transfer collaboration.
locator: Article body, fetched directly this session
rejection_reason: india_origin_exclusion: confirmed India-origin or India-only underlying technical claim; discovery/search lead only
notes: Named-buyer (NTPC, a majority state-owned Indian power utility) procurement evidence plus government incentive-program participation -- India market primary demand signal. | The raw scout's more specific SECI-SIGHT sub-claim (137 MW manufacturing plant, INR 202.76 crore / ~USD24.3M incentive) was not independently re-confirmed within this specific fetched article (which references SIGHT selection only in general terms) -- retained as unconfirmed supplementary detail from the scout's broader multi-source synthesis, not verified against a primary document this session. | Quarantined by 2026-07 India-origin rule; may be used only to discover an independent eligible source.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

title: First projects awarded funding under Japanese CfD hydrogen scheme
authors_or_org: Ammonia Energy Association (trade/industry association)
claim_supported: Ammonia Energy Association article (fetched): Japan selected its first two projects under its Contracts-for-Difference-style hydrogen/ammonia subsidy program -- a Toyota Tsusho-led green-hydrogen project supplying Aichi Steel's Chita-plant heating furnaces via onshore-wind-powered electrolysis, and a Resonac project producing low-carbon ammonia from hydrogen derived by gasifying waste plastics/textiles, supplying Nippon Shokubai, targeting an April 2030 operational start. The article frames the funding within Japan's broader 'GX' (Green Transformation) policy program, citing $51B earmarked for hydrogen/ammonia investment out of a $1T total GX scheme.
locator: Article body, fetched directly this session (substitutes for the raw scout's original fuelcellsworks.com URL, which returned HTTP 403 on repeated attempts this session)
notes: Original raw URL (fuelcellsworks.com) blocked (403) on two direct-fetch attempts; hydrogeninsight.com alternate coverage was paywall/SSO-gated. This substitute independently confirms the same underlying award event (same two named winners, same CfD program). Note: this source's '$51B of $1T GX scheme' framing differs from the JPY3T (~$19-20B), 15-year, hydrogen/ammonia-specific CfD figure cited via JOGMEC/METI reporting in L11-037 -- both figures are preserved rather than reconciled, per source-standards conflict discipline.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Ammonia Energy Association is a US-registered nonprofit (EIN 26-0821492) headquartered in Brooklyn, New York, US. Not India.

title: Sungrow Hydrogen electrolyser orders: 45 MW China order and 48 MW China Coal Pingshuo coal-mine deployment
authors_or_org: QC Intel / gasworld / Fuel Cells Works / RenewablesNow (trade press, reporting Sungrow Hydrogen contracts)
rejection_reason: inaccessible -- all fetch routes exhausted this session: qcintel.com (403), gasworld.com (403, two different article URLs), fuelcellsworks.com (403, two different article URLs), renewablesnow.com (403), hydrogeninsight.com (paywall/SSO redirect, two article URLs), web.archive.org (tool-level access denied in this environment). WebSearch snippets (not eligible as fetched=true evidence per source standards) corroborate a Sungrow-China Coal Pingshuo 48 MW order (eight 1,200 Nm3/h electrolyzers plus two 4,800 Nm3/h purification systems, Shuozhou, Shanxi, part of a 600 MW project) and a separate ~45 MW generic China order, but the raw scout's record conflated what appear to be two distinct Sungrow deals into a single claim without a successful primary fetch for either.
notes: Retained as rejected (not dropped) per verifier instructions to report all records including rejected ones. Should be re-attempted in a future session with different network routing, since the underlying facts appear well-corroborated across five independent trade outlets even though none could be directly fetched this session.
